reasons: EMERGENCY: diversity collapsed with frozen particles

turns.thought: [OBSERVE] Swarm is in diversity_collapse state: normalized diversity 1.32e-7, position std 3.6e-5, swarm radius 0.0033, 80% zero velocity, velocity direction consistency 1.0, fitness values nearly identical (std 1.2e-5). gbest is 4.81, far from global optimum 0. Improvement is negligible (relative 2.4e-6). Landscape label moderate_hills, ruggedness 0.47, high gradient magnitude (1.37e5), estimated basin radius 50.4, modality many. Swarm is tightly clustered in a local basin much smaller than landscape features. | [DIAGNOSE] Premature convergence to a deep local minimum on the highly multimodal Griewank landscape. The swarm has lost all diversity, velocities are frozen, and all particles are essentially at the same local optimum. The high gradient magnitude suggests steep walls around this basin, trapping the swarm. Without intervention, no further improvement is possible. | [STRATEGIZE] A strong diversity-restoring intervention is needed. Randomly reinitializing a large fraction of particles across the entire search space will break the current basin, inject fresh exploration, and disrupt the complete velocity alignment. Resetting velocities is essential to remove frozen state. Raising inertia weight slightly (1.1) encourages continued exploration after reinit. This is more effective than local perturbations or basin-hopping centered on the current gbest, which may not reach the global basin.
